✔ #1090 view - view the file at /home/arp/Documents/Xenovir/lib/auth/action.ts. 2835m 1s
/home/arp/Documents/Xenovir/lib/auth/action.ts
1. "use server";
2. 
3. import { headers } from "next/headers";
4. import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
5. import { createClient } from "@/lib/supabase/server";
6. 
7. export type ActionState = {
8.   success?: boolean;
9.   error?: string;
10.   message?: string;
11. } | null;
12. 
13. export async function checkSession() {
14.   const supabase = await createClient();
15.   const {
16.     data: { user },
17.   } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
18. 
19.   return { user };
20. }
21. 
22. export async function signout() {
23.   const supabase = await createClient();
24.   const { error } = await supabase.auth.signOut();
25. 
26.   if (error) {
27.     redirect("/error");
28.   }
29. 
30.   redirect("/auth/signout");
31. }
32. 
33. function getBaseUrl(headerStore: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof headers>>) {
34.   const forwardedHost = headerStore.get("x-forwarded-host");
35.   const forwardedProto = headerStore.get("x-forwarded-proto") ?? "https";
36. 
37.   if (forwardedHost) {
38.     return `${forwardedProto}://${forwardedHost}`;
39.   }
40. 
41.   let siteUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL;
42.   if (!siteUrl) {
43.     throw new Error(
44.       "Public site URL is not configured. Please set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL in your environment variables.",
45.     );
46.   }
47.   siteUrl = siteUrl.replace(/\/+$/, "");
48. 
49.   return siteUrl;
50. }
51. 
52. export async function updateProfile(
53.   _prevState: ActionState,
54.   formData: FormData,
55. ) {
56.   const supabase = await createClient();
57.   const fullName = (formData.get("full_name") as string)?.trim();
58.   const username = (formData.get("username") as string)?.trim();
59.   const nextPath = (formData.get("next") as string) || "/";
60. 
61.   if (!fullName || !username) {
62.     return { error: "All fields are required." };
63.   }
64. 
65.   if (fullName.length < 3 || fullName.length > 80) {
66.     return { error: "Full name must be between 3 and 80 characters long." };
67.   }
68. 
69.   if (username.length < 6 || username.length > 20) {
70.     return { error: "Username must be between 6 and 20 characters long." };
71.   }
72. 
73.   if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$/.test(username)) {
74.     return {
75.       error: "Username can only contain letters, and underscores.",
76.     };
77.   }
78. 
79.   const {
80.     data: { user },
81.   } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
82.   if (!user) return { error: "You must be logged in." };
83. 
84.   const { data: currentProfile } = await supabase
85.     .from("profiles")
86.     .select("username, last_modified")
87.     .eq("id", user.id)
88.     .maybeSingle();
89. 
90.   if (currentProfile?.last_modified) {
91.     const lastModifiedDate = new Date(currentProfile.last_modified).getTime();
92.     const sevenDaysInMs = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
93.     if (Date.now() - lastModifiedDate < sevenDaysInMs) {
94.       return { error: "Profile can only be updated once every 7 days." };
95.     }
96.   }
97. 
98.   const { data: existingUser } = await supabase
99.     .from("profiles")
100.     .select("id")
101.     .eq("username", username)
102.     .neq("id", user.id)
103.     .maybeSingle();
104. 
105.   if (existingUser) {
106.     return { error: "This username is already taken." };
107.   }
108. 
109.   const { error } = await supabase.from("profiles").upsert({
110.     id: user.id,
111.     full_name: fullName,
112.     username: username,
113.     email: user.email,
114.     last_modified: new Date().toISOString(),
115.   });
116. 
117.   if (error) {
118.     if (error.code === "23505") {
119.       return { error: "Username already exists." };
120.     }
121.     return { error: error.message };
122.   }
123. 
124.   redirect(nextPath);
125. }
126. 
127. export async function signUpNative(formData: FormData) {
128.   const supabase = await createClient();
129.   const email = formData.get("email") as string;
130.   const password = formData.get("password") as string;
131. 
132.   const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
133.     email,
134.     password,
135.     options: {
136.       emailRedirectTo: `${getBaseUrl(await headers())}/auth/callback?next=${encodeURIComponent("/auth/onboarding")}`,
137.     },
138.   });
139. 
140.   if (error) {
141.     return { error: error.message };
142.   }
143. 
144.   return { success: true, email };
145. }
146. 
147. export async function signInNative(formData: FormData) {
148.   const supabase = await createClient();
149. 
150.   const identifier = (formData.get("email") ||
151.     formData.get("username") ||
152.     formData.get("identifier")) as string;
153.   const password = formData.get("password") as string;
154.   const nextPath = (formData.get("next") as string) || "/";
155. 
156.   if (!identifier || !password) {
157.     return { error: "Alamat email/username dan kata sandi diwajibkan." };
158.   }
159. 
160.   let loginEmail = identifier;
161. 
162.   if (!identifier.includes("@")) {
163.     const { data, error: profileError } = await supabase
164.       .from("profiles")
165.       .select("email")
166.       .eq("username", identifier)
167.       .maybeSingle();
168. 
169.     if (profileError || !data?.email) {
170.       return { error: "Kredensial tidak valid." };
171.     }
172. 
173.     loginEmail = data.email;
174.   }
175. 
176.   const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({
177.     email: loginEmail,
178.     password,
179.   });
180. 
181.   if (error) {
182.     return { error: error.message };
183.   }
184. 
185.   redirect(nextPath);
186. }
187. 
188. export async function getCurrentUser() {
189.   const supabase = await createClient();
190.   const {
191.     data: { user },
192.   } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
193.   return user;
194. }
195. 
196. export async function getSession() {
197.   const supabase = await createClient();
198.   const {
199.     data: { user },
200.   } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
201.   return user;
202. }
203. 
204. export async function getProfileData(userId: string) {
205.   const supabase = await createClient();
206.   const { data: profileData } = await supabase
207.     .from("profiles")
208.     .select("full_name, username")
209.     .eq("id", userId)
210.     .single();
211.   return profileData;
212. }
213. 
214. export async function signOutAction() {
215.   const supabase = await createClient();
216.   await supabase.auth.signOut();
217. }
218.

💬 #1092 Copilot 2835m 4s

I see the issue! In the signInNative function, when the user enters a username (no @ symbol), it's querying the profiles table to find the email. This extra database query might be slow, especially if there's no index on the username field or if network latency is high.

Let me check the database schema and also look at the Supabase server client setup:

✔ #1093 view - view the file at /home/arp/Documents/Xenovir/supabase/migrations/001_create_reports_schema.sql. 2835m 6s
/home/arp/Documents/Xenovir/supabase/migrations/001_create_reports_schema.sql
1. -- Create report_types table
2. CREATE TABLE report_types (
3.   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
4.   name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
5.   description TEXT,
6.   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
7.   updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
8. );
9. 
10. -- Insert report types
11. INSERT INTO report_types (name, description) VALUES
12.   ('documentation', 'Documentation reports with photos and geolocation'),
13.   ('log', 'Activity log reports with timestamps and descriptions');
14. 
15. -- Create reports table
16. CREATE TABLE reports (
17.   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
18.   user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
19.   report_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES report_types(id),
20.   title TEXT NOT NULL,
21.   description TEXT,
22.   latitude NUMERIC(10, 8),
23.   longitude NUMERIC(11, 8),
24.   location_name TEXT,
25.   captured_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
26.   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
27.   updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
28. );
29. 
30. -- Create report_media table
31. CREATE TABLE report_media (
32.   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
33.   report_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
34.   file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
35.   file_type TEXT CHECK (file_type IN ('image', 'document')) DEFAULT 'image',
36.   captured_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
37.   order_index INTEGER DEFAULT 0
38. );
39. 
40. -- Create report_logs table for activity/log type reports
41. CREATE TABLE report_logs (
42.   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
43.   report_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
44.   activity TEXT NOT NULL,
45.   logged_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
46.   order_index INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
47.   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
48. );
49. 
50. -- Create report_metadata table for extensibility
51. CREATE TABLE report_metadata (
52.   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
53.   report_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES reports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
54.   key TEXT NOT NULL,
55.   value TEXT,
56.   UNIQUE(report_id, key)
57. );
58. 
59. -- Create indexes
60. CREATE INDEX reports_user_id_idx ON reports(user_id);
61. CREATE INDEX reports_report_type_id_idx ON reports(report_type_id);
62. CREATE INDEX reports_created_at_idx ON reports(created_at DESC);
63. CREATE INDEX report_media_report_id_idx ON report_media(report_id);
64. CREATE INDEX report_logs_report_id_idx ON report_logs(report_id);
65. CREATE INDEX report_metadata_report_id_idx ON report_metadata(report_id);
66.

💭 #1098 Reasoning 2835m 17s
I see! The profiles table doesn't have an index on the username field. When the user logs in with a username (no @ symbol), the query at line 163-167 in lib/auth/action.ts does: ```sql SELECT "email" FROM "profiles" WHERE "username" = identifier LIMIT 1 ``` Without an index on `username`, this query has to do a full table scan. If the profiles table has many rows, this could be slow. Let me add an index on the username field.
💬 #1099 Copilot 2835m 18s

Found it! The profiles table doesn't have an index on the username field. When you login with a username, the system has to scan the entire table to find your email. Let me add the missing index:

💬 #1108 Copilot 2836m 5s

I found the issue! 🔍 Your profiles table is missing an index on the username column. When you login with a username, the database has to scan every row to find your email - this causes the slowdown.

To fix this, run this SQL in your Supabase dashboard SQL editor:

CREATE INDEX profiles_username_idx ON profiles(username);

Steps:

  1. Go to your Supabase project dashboard

  2. Click SQL Editor on the left sidebar

  3. Click New Query

  4. Paste the SQL above

  5. Click Run

I've already updated the migration file for future setups. This index will make username lookups instant! 🚀 Once applied, login should be much faster.
